meaning: vt.使暴露,受到,使曝光v.揭露
examples: Altman strips away the pretense and mythology to expose the film industry as a business like any other.
My job as a journalist is to expose the truth.
He did not want to expose his fears and insecurity to anyone.
The front-page newspaper expose that would begin his downfall was still 14 weeks away.
To see a wrong and not expose it, is to become a silent partner to its continuance.
